item.title: Shell injection in generated bash tool wrappers — user-controlled vars interpolated without safe quoting
item.intent: Eliminate command injection risk in all generated tool scripts. Every variable must be safely quoted or the tool generation must switch to a safer language.
item.description: Code review found that generated bash scripts in src/design/tools/ (email.ts, todoist.ts, ical.ts, etc.) interpolate user-controlled variables like $local_body and $local_id into shell commands without proper quoting. An attacker-controlled value containing shell metacharacters can break escaping and execute arbitrary commands inside the agent container.
steps: Audit all tool generators in src/design/tools/ for unquoted variable expansions
Apply safe quoting (double-quote all variable refs) or switch to Python with shlex
Add tests that generate tools with metacharacter-laden inputs and verify no injection
acceptanceCriteria: All generated tool scripts use proper shell quoting for every variable expansion
No unquoted variable interpolation exists in any tool generator output
Alternatively, tool generation switches to Python with shlex for safe argument handling
